reasoning:
The 4-week moving average of continued claims was 1,947,500 for the week ending October 25, 2025 (actually 2024 equivalent). This elevated level captures the initial surges in unemployment due to recent hurricanes (Helene/Milton) and the Boeing strike. 
For 2025-11-02 (horizon 1 week): The 4-week MA will drop a pre-hurricane week (Oct 4) and add a high post-hurricane week (Nov 1). Thus, it is mathematically very highly likely to increase from the Oct 25 value.
For 2025-11-25 (horizon 1 month): The temporary shocks from the hurricanes and strikes will likely be fading or resolved, bringing weekly continued claims back toward their baseline (which was closer to 1,920,000 in September). This suggests the 4-week MA will peak and then fall below the 1,947,500 threshold by late November.
For 2026-01-24 (horizon 3 months): By late January, temporary effects will be fully washed out. However, considering the gradual underlying upward trend in the baseline over the past year (about +300 per day), baseline claims could drift back up near the threshold of 1.948 million. Thus, the probability for this distant horizon is set extremely close to 50%.
